title: triticum pungens subsp. obtusiusculum
intro: Triticum pungens subsp. obtusiusculum, also known as wild einkorn, is a subspecies of the wild einkorn wheat plant, a type of ancient grain native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to act as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. The health benefits of wild einkorn include its high nutritional value, rich in fiber, protein, and minerals, making it a nutritious addition to a healthy diet. Wild einkorn can be consumed as a grain, used to make flour for baking, or prepared as an herbal tea, with some herbalists recommending it as a treatment for digestive issues and as a way to support overall well-being.
